metrics.narrative: Omnia has repeatedly quantified the problem it is buying against: an entirely manual, Excel-based procurement governance process across roughly ZAR 16 billion (~USD 1 billion) of spend, with 15 sourcing events and 18 contracts in flight simultaneously (Source to the relevant stakeholder of Work call, 19 Feb 2026). A separate, persistent metric is the 3-week lag between month-close and usable spend data, which Marianne Ventura called a 'significant frustration' and said could not wait (SOW discussion and closure call, 30 Jun 2026, ~9 days ago). This evidence is rich and recent but sits against the account's S2C24 history rather than logged activity on this specific opportunity record.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-30 (SOW discussion and closure call)
champion.narrative: Marianne Ventura is a strong, active champion: she drove the executive business-case session (Oct 2025), personally walked every SOW redline to closure on the 30 Jun 2026 call, and stated 'I never wanna do another one of these by email ever again' and that Omnia has 'to report we're ready to sign.' She is, however, the single consistently visible driver across the whole trail; Zama Khambula, Dieter Sparbier and Tracy Seeley-Jeppe appear only episodically.

competition.narrative: The only named competitor across the entire read history is Adobe Sign, the incumbent e-signature tool Omnia was using without a formal workflow (60% adoption, no integration) which Certinal has now displaced in the finalized SOW. An early call (24 Apr 2025) noted Omnia was 'exploring a source-to-contract system and considering Zycus among other options,' but no rival S2P/S2C vendor was ever named in a subsequent call.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-30 (SOW discussion and closure call)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well established and consistent across nine months of calls: fully manual Excel-based procurement governance, 60% adoption of Adobe Sign with no workflow integration, a legacy supplier master with gaps traced back to a 2020 cyber incident, and a recurring 3-week delay in usable spend data. Marianne Ventura's own words capture the urgency: 'these are very time consuming for someone like me.'

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-30 (SOW discussion and closure call)
paper_process.narrative: Paper process is well documented and matches the standard Zycus contracting relay: MSA, Order Form and SOW were being finalized, Certinal was confirmed as the e-signature platform (replacing the earlier 'TBD'), spend threshold was raised to ZAR 1 billion at no commercial uplift, and country phasing was trimmed (Indonesia and DRC removed due to legacy QAD/AX2012 constraints). As of 30 Jun 2026, Marianne Ventura said she needed one more day to route the SOW through internal legal sign-off (Adobe Sign, for audit trail) before sending it to Zycus for external signature.

sources: Avoma call 2025-10-13 (FW: Source to Contract Demo and Business Case)
economic_buyer.narrative: An unnamed Omnia legal director/board member and Jacques de Villiers (Group Head of Supply Chain, per Salesforce) were named as the two sign-off authorities whose approval unlocks budget, and both were direct attendees of the 13 Oct 2025 executive business-case presentation ('his signing off on this is important to me, along with Jacques de Villiers'). That is now roughly 9 months old (>180 days, heavily decayed) and no direct executive re-engagement is evidenced since; all subsequent progress has run through the champion. Status is partial: the economic buyer is named and was directly engaged once, but not recently.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-30 (SOW discussion and closure call)
decision_process.narrative: The process traced clearly from the Oct 2025 executive business-case session through SOW scoping (Feb 2026), e-signature selection (Mar 2026), onboarding (Apr 2026), design-document review (Jun 2026) to a final SOW redline closure call on 30 Jun 2026, where Marianne Ventura walked line-by-line through every open comment and closed nearly all of them, then said 'we're ready to sign.'

sources: Avoma call 2026-03-18 (Zycus with Omnia: Modules Walkthrough Session)
Avoma call 2026-06-30 (SOW discussion and closure call)
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria were built out in detail across a module walkthrough (18 Mar 2026), a design-document walkthrough (23 Jun 2026), and the final SOW closure call (30 Jun 2026): approval-workflow depth (5 levels sourcing, up to 10 for contracting/supplier onboarding), supplier risk/sanctions screening coverage, spend-analytics frequency, and integration pattern preference (event-based primary, API fallback) were all explicitly negotiated and mostly resolved into the SOW.

items.name: SOW discussion and closure call
items.summary: Champion resolved nearly all open SOW redlines, confirmed Certinal, raised spend threshold to ZAR 1bn at no extra cost, and removed Indonesia/DRC from rollout scope.

day_summary.overall: The most recent real deal activity found anywhere on this account was the SOW discussion and closure call on 2026-06-30 (about 9 days before this review). Marianne Ventura worked through nearly every open SOW comment with the Zycus team, confirmed Certinal as the e-signature platform, accepted a spend-threshold increase with no commercial uplift, trimmed Indonesia and DRC from the rollout, and committed to completing internal legal sign-off before routing documents to Zycus for countersignature. No activity of any kind is logged against this specific opportunity record in the 9 days since, and today's date (2026-07-09) shows no further confirmation that signature was completed.
